independent_of.note: This module never imports or reads the identity-crosswalk module or artifact, and no availability status is conditioned on identity-resolution status.

mirror_source_commit_pin.note: MIRROR_SOURCE_COMMIT_PIN -- the commit that merged PR #159 (Lane A); the CLI dereferences the wrapper, mirror files, and mirror directory listing at exactly this commit via git show/git ls-tree, never the current worktree

hard_rejection_decision.decided: after two independent review rounds on PR #161, following a user decision to hard-block rather than design full per-field_family structured evidence contracts now
what_is_hard_rejected: any row's availability_status of eligible_at_cutoff or ineligible_after_cutoff, unconditionally, regardless of how well-evidenced
any row's available_at, evidence_source, or review_decision being non-null
any row's source_snapshot_as_of being non-null (no reproducible snapshot-evidence contract exists)
the artifact's cutoff_at or cutoff_evidence_source being non-null (they exist solely to support the two hard-rejected statuses)
why: record-level binding gap: proving a row's claimed value and timestamp come from the SAME source record for THIS player/field_family (not just two independent substrings found somewhere in a reproduced archive) requires per-field_family structured evidence contracts, and for derived families (age_at_entry, college_production) deterministic recomputation from underlying raw data -- none of which is designed yet

semantic-role gap: a string match proves a timestamp appears in an archive, not that it specifically means 'this fact became publicly knowable' as opposed to an event/retrieval/unrelated timestamp; the cutoff proof has the analogous gap for 'this is the 2026 Day 1/Round 1 start'
hard_rejection_decision.precedent: mirrors how Lane A ultimately hard-rejected its own unverifiable 3.3_governed_artifact evidence class (#159) rather than continuing to accept mechanically-plausible-but-unauthoritative citations
hard_rejection_decision.practical_consequence: ..._complete is not reachable for this population under schema 1.0.0, since every row with a present mirror value is permanently unresolved_no_availability_proof until a future schema version designs and lifts this hard-rejection
hard_rejection_decision.follow_up: tracked under issue #160: design a per-field_family structured evidence contract (with deterministic recomputation for derived families) as separate follow-up work, then lift the hard-rejection in a future schema version

design_history_superseded_by_hard_block: proactive self-certification fix (before any review): mirrored_value_literal cross-checked against the real pinned mirror value, not just the row's own cited archive
automated review pass: available_at bound into the archive-content check; wrapper source_lock comparison included commit

owner review round 1: same-archive-different-timestamp-same-side and bare-date-archive regressions; mirror_source.commit dereferenced via git show rather than shape-checked; wrapper forecast_mirror.paths checked exactly; cutoff timezone/instant rigor; top-level schema closure

owner review round 2: mirror directory listing dereferenced at the pinned commit via git ls-tree; source_timezone_or_offset closed to numeric offsets only; source_snapshot_as_of hard-rejected as non-null; nested schema closure; temporal chronology (retrieval/review/availability ordering)

each round closed a real gap and is preserved in the module's git history, but each was also shown to still be a mechanical proxy for record-level/semantic proof rather than the genuine thing -- this directly informed the conclusion that a fundamentally different evidence contract, not a further-hardened generic one, is what closing eligible_at_cutoff/ineligible_after_cutoff honestly requires

validator_enforcement.contract_pins: kind/schema_version/issue/governing_design (readiness-design issue/PR/merge-commit/document paths)/season match pinned constants exactly; top-level artifact fields, and every nested object (source_identity, mirror_source, governing_design, status_counts, status_counts_by_family), must match an exact closed field set
validator_enforcement.mirror_dereferencing: mirror_source repo/pinned-commit-exact-match (MIRROR_SOURCE_COMMIT_PIN)/path/kind/schema_version checked, sha256 must equal the recomputed hash of the actual wrapper file at that commit; the dereferenced wrapper's source_lock (including commit), declared forecast_mirror.paths (exact match), the pinned-commit mirror directory listing (via git ls-tree, not a live-worktree readdir), and mirrored_hashes are all independently recomputed and matched
validator_enforcement.value_presence_agreement: availability_status is unavailable iff the real pinned mirror value for (player, field_family) is null, checked directly against the mirror JSON
validator_enforcement.population_accounting: exactly 240 distinct governed (identity, family) keys; every locked identity has all 5 families; no extra identity or family; declared status_counts and status_counts_by_family recomputed and must match, summing to 240/48 respectively
validator_enforcement.eligible_ineligible_hard_rejection: any row claiming eligible_at_cutoff or ineligible_after_cutoff is rejected outright, unconditionally -- see hard_rejection_decision
validator_enforcement.row_field_nullability: available_at, evidence_source, review_decision, and source_snapshot_as_of must be null on every row; notes must be string | null
validator_enforcement.artifact_field_nullability: cutoff_at and cutoff_evidence_source must be null on the artifact

cutoff_section_8.detail: no archived, dated NFL draft-schedule evidence was assembled, and no human review of a cutoff candidate occurred. cutoff_at and cutoff_evidence_source are hard-required null in schema 1.0.0 regardless.

per_row_exact_value_evidence_sections_10.detail: no archived dated snapshot evidence was assembled for any of the 240 pairs. even if it had been, eligible_at_cutoff/ineligible_after_cutoff are hard-rejected outright in schema 1.0.0.

missingness_vs_timing_section_11.detail: 17 (player, field_family) pairs carry a null value in the committed mirror itself -- a missingness fact, independent of any timing question. the remaining 223 pairs have a non-null pinned value but no availability-timing evidence (and no timing status is currently usable regardless), recorded unresolved_no_availability_proof.

accounting.unavailable_rows_are_exactly: the (player, field_family) pairs where the committed mirror's own value is null -- 1 player missing age_at_entry, 16 players missing athletic_testing; no other family has any missing value in the locked population

confirmations.no_human_review_claimed_or_claimable: review_decision, available_at, evidence_source, and source_snapshot_as_of are hard-required null on every row; cutoff_at/cutoff_evidence_source are hard-required null on the artifact

validation.tests: npm test -- full suite passes (1260 tests across the repo), including 59 in tests/rookieTransitionProfileAvailabilityEvidence.test.ts
test_coverage_summary: committed artifact passes validation against the real, pinned-commit-dereferenced mirror
kind/schema/design pins
exact 240-row population, every timing/evidence field null, eight-field row contract
requires_followup decision and full accounting
missing identity (all five rows for a locked player removed)
missing single family for an otherwise-present identity
extra row for an unlocked identity
duplicate governed key
invalid field_family / availability_status tokens
extra or missing row field
out-of-order rows
tampered status_counts / status_counts_by_family
tampered issue reference and governing-design merge commit

eligible_at_cutoff/ineligible_after_cutoff hard-rejected: for a row with a present real mirror value even with every other field properly null, for both statuses, for official_postdraft_outcome specifically, and confirming ..._complete is never reachable if every row is relabeled eligible

non-null timing/evidence fields hard-rejected: available_at, evidence_source, review_decision (any row, not only unavailable/unresolved), cutoff_at, cutoff_evidence_source, any non-null source_snapshot_as_of
value-presence agreement in both directions, with a real-null-value control case
nested schema closure: extra key on governing_design, mirror_source, source_identity, status_counts (outer and per-family), and a non-string/non-null notes
top-level schema closure: extra/missing top-level field, unparseable generated_at, generated_at_is_operational_timestamp_only_not_fact_availability not exactly true, with a control case

mirror-wrapper dereferencing checks (tampered sha256, wrong/substituted-but-well-formed commit, wrong repo, mismatched recomputed hashes, unauthorized extra mirror file via the pinned-commit git ls-tree listing, tampered source_lock including a moved commit, substituted/missing forecast_mirror.paths entries, with a control case)
malformed locked-population size
two inertness scans
validation.audit_cli: npm run audit:rookie-transition-profile-availability-evidence -- valid: true with the accounting above, built by dereferencing the wrapper, mirror files, and mirror directory listing at the exact pinned Forecast commit via git show/git ls-tree

decision_rationale: The governed Lane B artifact, fail-closed validator, tests, and full 240-row accounting are in place and passing. 223 of 240 (player, field_family) pairs are honestly unresolved_no_availability_proof and the remaining 17 are honestly unavailable; no row claims eligible_at_cutoff or ineligible_after_cutoff, both of which are hard-rejected outright in schema 1.0.0 pending a per-field_family structured evidence contract. ..._complete is not reachable for this population until that follow-up design exists and a future schema version lifts the hard-rejection.
decision_authorizes: a separate follow-up under issue #160 to design a per-field_family structured evidence contract (with deterministic recomputation for derived families) and a typed semantic role for availability/cutoff timestamps
a future schema version lifting the eligible_at_cutoff/ineligible_after_cutoff hard-rejection once that contract exists
